settings.title: 100 Days Return
settings.description:
Enjoy a seamless shopping experience, facilitated
by our complimentary shipping.

settings.title: Lifetime Warranty
settings.description:
Our dedicated team provides timely assistance,
committed to addressing your queries.

settings.title: Customers Services
settings.description: Invest in our activewear with absolute confidence, backed by our 30-day satisfaction guarantee.

settings.content:
Shipping cost is based on weight. Just add products to your cart and use the Shipping Calculator to see the shipping price.

We want you to be 100% satisfied with your purchase. Items can be returned or exchanged within 30 days of delivery.

settings.header: How long does it take to process an order?
settings.content: All the details you love about the original—with a fresh flared block heel and modern square toe. Expertly crafted using soft Nappa leather, this essential style molds and moves with you to keep you comfortably on your feet all day (and night). This boot uses leather sourced from a Leather Working Group-approved trader. The Leather Working Group (LWG) Audit Standards provide transparency and accountability within the leather supply chain—covering energy and water usage.

settings.name: Lamb's Tongue Round
settings.description: It resists dents and creases, cleans easily, and allows steady airflow without glare or shine. A reliable, all-purpose option that suits most homes and climates.

settings.name: Square Edge
settings.description: If you’d prefer a more angular look, choose the square edge profile.

settings.name: Soft Ogee
settings.description: A smaller-scale ogee with a soft S-curve that adds subtle historic detail without overwhelming the lighter proportions of storm and screen doors. Used for fixed-glass construction.

settings.edge_profile_description: Removable inserts snap securely in and out of the wood surround built into your porch panel, held by discreet interior clips — no tools required.

settings.name: Knotty Alder
settings.description: Rustic charm for country homes or cabins. Use in covered areas — not recommended for full exposure to rain or sun.

settings.name: Knotty Pine
settings.description: A softwood with vintage appeal. Great for protected porches on historic homes, but must be kept sealed and covered.

settings.name: Eastern White Pine
settings.description: Budget-friendly and easy to paint or stain. Only recommended for interior-facing or fully enclosed porch entries.

settings.name: Southern Yellow Pine
settings.description: Heavier and stronger than Eastern Pine. Holds paint well — best used in covered or protected settings.

settings.name: Poplar
settings.description: Smooth, stable, and ideal for painted screen doors in fully enclosed or interior porch areas.

settings.name: Black Walnut
settings.description: Striking grain and deep color, but sensitive to sun and moisture. Best for covered entries or deep-overhang porches.

settings.name: Quarter Sawn White Oak
settings.description: Steady vertical grain lit by silvery rays and flecks. A premium, historically accurate, and remarkably stable wood that holds up well in temperature-changing conditions.

settings.name: Cherry
settings.description: Traditional and warm-toned. Ages beautifully on covered porches where it’s protected from direct sun and rain.

settings.name: Red Oak
settings.description: Familiar hardwood with visible grain. Use only in enclosed or semi-protected settings — not ideal for full weather exposure.

settings.name: Maple
settings.description: Dense and smooth. Works well for storm doors in protected areas where a clean, refined look is preferred.

settings.name: White Oak
settings.description: Tougher than red oak and more moisture-resistant. A dependable choice for storm doors that face seasonal elements.

settings.name: Ash
settings.description: Durable and shock-resistant. Ideal for storm doors in high-traffic areas that take daily use and occasional knocks.

settings.name: African Mahogany
settings.description: Vintage Doors Recommended. Our top pick for screen doors exposed to wind, rain, or cold. Dense, stable, and naturally rot-resistant — holds up without warping.

settings.name: Vertical Grain Douglas Fir
settings.description: A porch classic. Straight-grained, stable, and long-trusted for screen doors in damp or shifting climates like the Northeast.

settings.name: Honduran Mahogany
settings.description: Strong, refined, and weather-tough. A luxury wood that performs beautifully through seasonal temperature and moisture swings.

settings.name: Spanish Cedar
settings.description: Lightweight with a fine grain and soft color. Naturally insect- and moisture-resistant — great for coastal areas or wooded settings. Accepts both stain and paint.

settings.name: Western Red Cedar
settings.description: Naturally beautiful with a soft, relaxed look. Ideal for cottage-style homes and cabins. Resists rot even when unfinished.

settings.any_description: These beautiful woods are excellent choices for doors in any location: fully exposed, partially exposed, or fully protected (indoor) locations. Due to their weather resistance and durability, these woods are required for doors that will be exposed to the elements: sun, rain, and snow.
settings.partial_description: These woods are suitable for doors in either partially exposed locations—locations with some protection from the elements—or fully protected, indoor locations.
settings.protected_description: These additional wood options are available for protected indoor locations, away from any weather exposure or the effects of excessive heat, cold or moisture.

settings.title: Additional Options
settings.text:
Top & Bottom Inserts

Oversized doors can include a split (two-piece) glass insert for easier handling or quick screen replacement.

Fixed Screen

Rather than removable inserts, you can opt to have glass permanently built into the door openings on select storm door models.

settings.title: Additional Options
settings.text:
Top & Bottom Inserts

Oversized doors can include a split (two-piece) insert for easier handling or quick screen replacement.

Fixed Screen

Rather than removable inserts, you can opt to have screens permanently built into the door openings on select screen door models.

settings.tab_one_sub_description: Let in fresh air in while keeping bugs and weather out
settings.tab_one_description:
Removable inserts snap securely in and out of the wood surround built into your porch panel, held by discreet interior clips — no tools required.

Each insert is framed in ¾" aluminum, finished in white or bronze to complement your panel’s design.

Screen options include Fiberglass, Aluminum, Copper (Bronze), Pet Screens, and No-See-Um screens. You’ll select your screen type while configuring your door.
settings.tab_two_sub_description: Let in fresh air in while keeping bugs and weather out
settings.tab_two_description:
Screen inserts can be removable or permanent, depending on how you plan to use your porch.

Removable inserts snap securely in and out of the wood surround built into your porch panel, held by discreet interior clips — no tools required.

Each insert is framed in ¾" aluminum, finished in white or bronze to complement your panel’s design.

Screen options include Fiberglass, Aluminum, Copper (Bronze), Pet Screens, and No-See-Um screens. You’ll select your screen type while configuring your door.

settings.modal_title: How To Measure for Your Door
settings.tab_1_subheading: Use this only if your current door fits well and swings smoothly.
settings.tab_1_description:
No need to remove the door — just measure:

  • Width: Edge to edge across the door face
  • Height: Top edge to bottom edge (not the sill)
  • Thickness: Door edge where the latch is

Record as: Width × Height × Thickness 

Example: 32" × 80" × 1¾"

We’ll build the new door to match these exact dimensions.

If your old door rubs or sticks: 

Skip this method and measure the finished opening instead.
settings.tab_2_subheading: Use this when you have an existing frame and just need a door that fits.
settings.tab_2_description:
You’re measuring the clear space inside the frame, where the door actually moves. You’ll take four measurements:

Left Height (A–B)

Inside top jamb to floor or sill

Right Height (C–D)

Inside top jamb to floor or sill
(Heights may differ; this is normal.)

Top Width (E–F)

Inside left jamb to inside right jamb

Bottom Width (G–H)

Inside left jamb to inside right jamb
(Floors can slope; widths may vary.)

Record as: A–B, C–D, E–F, G–H

Example: 80¼" | 80" | 31⅞" | 32"

We’ll size your new door slightly under these numbers for a clean field fit.

Tip: Measure only the inner wooden frame, not the casing or trim.
settings.tab_3_subheading: Use this for new builds or when replacing a full pre-hung unit (door + jamb).
settings.tab_3_description:
A rough opening is simply the unfinished space framed by studs.

Measure:

  • Width: Stud to stud
  • Height: Subfloor to underside of header
  • Depth: Wall thickness to confirm jamb depth

Record as: Width × Height × Depth

Example: 34½" × 82" × 4⅝"

If you see bare studs, you’re in the right place.
If you see painted or stained trim, use the finished opening method instead.
settings.tab_4_heading: A Pre-Hung Screen & Storm Door on a Jamb
settings.tab_4_subheading: Choose this option when your new screen & storm door will include a jamb.
settings.tab_4_description:
Choose this option when your new door will include a jamb. This applies to porch and patio openings, breezeways, or any situation where there is no existing jamb to reuse.

Measure the rough opening:

  • Width: Stud to stud
  • Height: Subfloor to header
  • Depth: Wall thickness

Record as: Width × Height × Depth

Example: 32½" × 82" × 4⅝"
settings.tab_5_heading: A Screen & Storm Door on Brick Molding
settings.tab_5_subheading: Use this method when your new screen or storm door mounts in front of your main entrance door, not inside the existing interior jam
settings.tab_5_description:
You’ll take four measurements:

Left Height (A–B)

Inside top jamb to floor or sill

Right Height (C–D)

Inside top jamb to floor or sill
(Heights may differ — this is normal.)

Top Width (E–F)

Inside left jamb to inside right jamb

Bottom Width (G–H)

Inside left jamb to inside right jamb
(Floors can slope; widths may vary.)

Record as:

A–B, C–D, E–F, G–H
Example: 80¼" | 80" | 31⅞" | 32"
